kill_fact: No kill_fact found. The bear thesis survives every attack against primary sources: NNE is pre-revenue with ZERO binding offtake (Supermicro/BaRupOn/EHC/DS Dansuk are all non-binding MOUs), the UIUC KRONOS Construction Permit Application is confirmed to be a Part 50 PROTOTYPE/research-reactor filing (commercial fleet needs the future Part 53/57 framework, not yet pursued), the $300-350M FOAK cost is unvalidated against $568.7M cash, and base-case SoP (~$24) sits BELOW spot (~$28) so the AI-power optionality is already priced. Verdict C / WATCHLIST holds.
corrections: (1) STALE-ANCHOR / fresher milestone: the debate frames the CPA as merely 'filed in 2026'; primary source confirms the CPA was submitted 2026-03-31 AND the NRC FORMALLY ACCEPTED it for review on 2026-05-20 (a dated, post-debate docketing milestone) — modestly bull-supportive, de-risks acceptance, but does NOT move the ~2030 finish line or change the verdict. (2) ATM OVERSTATEMENT: bear says the '$400M ATM GUARANTEES dilution' — the 10-Q explicitly states 'no Placement Shares have been sold' as of the report, so the ATM is ARMED but UNDRAWN; the $384M of FY26 financing came from an Oct-2025 institutional private placement, NOT the ATM. Dilution risk is real and forward, not yet realized. (3) The Part 50 (prototype) vs commercial-license gap, $568.7M cash, $9.18M quarterly net loss, $9.25M 6-mo operating cash burn, and 52,083,294 shares all VERIFY exactly against the 10-Q.
evidence: NNE 10-Q (filed via SEC, period ended 2026-03-31, on StockTitan): 52,083,294 shares outstanding; $197,675,624 cash + $370,997,289 ST investments = $568.7M liquidity; net cash used in operating activities $9,254,201 (6mo); Q2 net loss $9,180,166; 2025 ATM Program max $400M with 'no Placement Shares have been sold'; S-3 shelf effective 2026-03-13.

GlobeNewswire 2026-05-20: 'NANO Nuclear's KRONOS MMR and University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign Advance to Next Regulatory Milestone as U.S. NRC Formally Accepts Construction Permit Application for Review' — CPA submitted 2026-03-31, NRC review estimated complete in 2027.

GlobeNewswire 2026-04-28: 'NANO Nuclear Sees KRONOS MMR Well-Aligned with NRC's Evolving Advanced Reactor Frameworks Under Part 53 and Proposed Part 57' — confirms UIUC KRONOS deployment proceeds under existing Part 50 research-reactor framework; Part 53/57 efficiencies apply to LATER commercial deployments, not the FOAK prototype.

Motley Fool 2026-06-20 ('NRC Formally Accepted Nano Nuclear's Construction Permit Application'): confirms acceptance is procedural docketing (begin review), not approval; commercialization timeline remains ~2030.
